# Resolution Correctness Checklist: SPEC-023 - OCaml Language Support

**Purpose**: Validate that SPEC-023 resolution-correctness requirements are complete, clear, consistent, and measurable before implementation tasks are generated.
**Created**: 2026-07-05
**Feature**: [spec.md](../spec.md)

**Note**: This checklist is generated by the `/speckit-checklist` command based on feature context and requirements.

## Requirement Completeness

- [ ] CHK001 Are module-path resolution requirements complete for qualified references, nested modules, local opens, includes, and shadowed names? [Completeness, Spec §FR-006, Spec §Edge Cases, Contract §Relationship Output]
- [ ] CHK002 Are open and include scoping requirements complete for file-level, nested-module, local-open, and signature-include cases without requiring typechecker-grade semantics? [Completeness, Spec §FR-006, Research §Resolution is Dune-scoped, Data Model §Module Candidate]
- [ ] CHK003 Are Dune workspace-root and package-boundary requirements complete for `dune-project`, `dune` stanzas, root `*.opam`, and `opam/*.opam` metadata? [Completeness, Spec §FR-008, Data Model §Workspace Metadata, Quickstart §3]
- [ ] CHK004 Are functor reference requirements specified for functor applications, functor argument modules, result-module aliases, and ambiguous functor targets? [Completeness, Spec §FR-006, Research §Resolution is Dune-scoped, Contract §Relationship Output]
- [ ] CHK005 Are `.ml`/`.mli` join requirements complete for unique pairs, source-only files, interface-only files, and ambiguous same-basename candidates? [Completeness, Spec §FR-007, Research §.ml and .mli pairing, Data Model §OCaml Interface Pairing]
- [ ] CHK006 Are package metadata requirements complete enough to constrain local relationships while excluding package nodes, external package edges, installed switches, lock directories, and network package state? [Completeness, Spec §FR-008, Spec §FR-009, Contract §Fail-Closed Behavior]

## Requirement Clarity

- [ ] CHK007 Is "Dune-scoped" defined with objective local sources and workspace/package boundaries rather than implicit directory proximity? [Clarity, Spec §Clarifications, Data Model §Workspace Metadata, Contract §Relationship Output]
- [ ] CHK008 Is "unique-only" clarified so the resolver must emit no edge unless exactly one candidate survives all source, pairing, and metadata constraints? [Clarity, Spec §FR-006, Spec §FR-009, Research §Resolution is Dune-scoped]
- [ ] CHK009 Is fail-closed behavior for ambiguous module, package, `.ml`/`.mli`, and PPX cases stated as omission of precision rather than fallback ranking? [Clarity, Spec §FR-009, Contract §Fail-Closed Behavior]
- [ ] CHK010 Are package metadata limits clear that metadata gates local relationships only and never creates `package` nodes or external package edges? [Clarity, Spec §FR-008, Spec §FR-009, Research §Package metadata constrains local relationships only]
- [ ] CHK011 Are relationship edge kinds and evidence sources clear enough to distinguish containment, imports, references, interface pairing, open/include scope, and checked-in metadata? [Clarity, Data Model §OCaml Relationship, Contract §Relationship Output]

## Requirement Consistency

- [ ] CHK012 Do spec, plan, research, data model, contract, and quickstart agree that resolution is local, deterministic, Dune-scoped, and unique-only? [Consistency, Spec §FR-006, Plan §Summary, Research §Resolution is Dune-scoped, Quickstart §3]
- [ ] CHK013 Do all artifacts consistently exclude PPX expansion, generated-code inference, package nodes, external package edges, installed switch state, and network package metadata? [Consistency, Spec §FR-009, Spec §FR-014, Plan §Constraints, Contract §Non-Contract]
- [ ] CHK014 Are open and include described consistently as conservative relationship evidence rather than standalone public symbols? [Consistency, Spec §Clarifications, Contract §Symbol Output, Contract §Relationship Output]
- [ ] CHK015 Are source/interface pairing requirements consistent between symbol exposure, module resolution constraints, and user-visible search behavior? [Consistency, Spec §FR-007, Data Model §OCaml Interface Pairing, Contract §Relationship Output]

## Acceptance Criteria Quality

- [ ] CHK016 Can SC-004 objectively measure zero speculative relationships for ambiguous package, module, source/interface, and PPX cases? [Acceptance Criteria, Spec §SC-004, Quickstart §4, Contract §Validation Evidence Contract]
- [ ] CHK017 Can graph-stability requirements objectively identify node or edge explosion from open/include expansion, functor-heavy modules, package metadata, or repeated indexing? [Acceptance Criteria, Spec §FR-016, Spec §SC-002, Data Model §Validation Corpus]
- [ ] CHK018 Are success criteria tied to deterministic probe evidence for resolution-heavy structural questions instead of only fixture-level assertions? [Acceptance Criteria, Spec §SC-003, Research §Nine retrieval questions, Quickstart §5]

## Edge Case Coverage

- [ ] CHK019 Are multi-package workspace cases covered where identical module names exist in separate local scopes or packages? [Coverage, Spec §Edge Cases, Data Model §Workspace Metadata]
- [ ] CHK020 Are ambiguity cases covered for duplicate module candidates, duplicate interface pairings, package metadata that cannot decide a local relationship, unsupported PPX, and generated-code-only references? [Coverage, Spec §FR-009, Contract §Fail-Closed Behavior]
- [ ] CHK021 Are open/include edge cases covered for shadowed names, nested modules, local opens, and includes inside structures or signatures? [Coverage, Spec §Edge Cases, OCaml Manual §Module system]

## Dependencies & Assumptions

- [ ] CHK022 Are authoritative metadata dependencies limited to checked-in Dune and opam files with ignored sources explicitly documented? [Dependency, Spec §Clarifications, Research §Package metadata constrains local relationships only, Dune Docs §package, opam Manual §Package definitions]
- [ ] CHK023 Are typechecker-grade module elaboration, OCaml LSP precision, and PPX expansion excluded while still requiring conservative source-level visibility? [Scope, Spec §Assumptions, Plan §Summary, Contract §Non-Contract]
